free_text: Energy dependence of the deuteron elastic scattering
for nuclei in the region of A = 40
Data taken from the report.
U-120 cm cyclotron
For the detection of scattered deuterons.
The energy resolution of about 200 keV.
In addition, a scintillation beam monitor
looking at a thin gold target, placed in front of the
main target, was applied.
The beam current was measured by means of a
current integrator.
In order to check the estimation of the absolute values
of cross-sections the elastic scattering on Au nuclei
at small scattering angles was also measured by means
of the same detecting system.
The relative errors were about 3% in
angular range from 40 to 110 degree, and up to 8% for
smallest and largest angles.
The errors in absolute cross-section values
were estimated for Al, Ca, and Ti (10%) and for S (15%)
The inaccuracy in the angle estimations due
to errors in the angle settings and in the beam
collimation  (0.2%)
